class FenwickTree
  def initialize(size)
    @n = size
    @tree = Array.new(@n + 1, 0)
  end

  def update(index, delta)
    i = index
    while i <= @n
      @tree[i] += delta
      i += i & -i
    end
  end

  def query(index)
    res = 0
    i = index
    while i > 0
      res += @tree[i]
      i -= i & -i
    end
    res
  end

  def range_query(l, r)
    query(r) - query(l - 1)
  end
end

def compute_depth(x)
  depth = 0
  while x != 1
    x = x.to_s(2).count('1')
    depth += 1
  end
  depth
end

def solve(nums, queries)
  n = nums.length
  depths = Array.new(n) { |i| compute_depth(nums[i]) }

  fenwick_trees = Array.new(6) { FenwickTree.new(n) }

  depths.each_with_index do |d, i|
    fenwick_trees[d].update(i + 1, 1)
  end

  answer = []
  queries.each do |q|
    if q[0] == 1
      l = q[1]
      r = q[2]
      k = q[3]
      if k < 0 || k > 5
        answer << 0
        next
      end
      ft = fenwick_trees[k]
      count = ft.range_query(l + 1, r + 1)
      answer << count
    else
      idx = q[1]
      val = q[2]
      old_val = nums[idx]
      old_depth = depths[idx]
      new_depth = compute_depth(val)
      fenwick_trees[old_depth].update(idx + 1, -1)
      fenwick_trees[new_depth].update(idx + 1, 1)
      nums[idx] = val
      depths[idx] = new_depth
    end
  end
  answer
end

class FenwickTree
  def initialize(size)
    @n = size
    @tree = Array.new(@n + 1, 0)
  end

  def update(index, delta)
    # index is 1-based
    i = index
    while i <= @n
      @tree[i] += delta
      i += i & -i
    end
  end

  def query(index)
    # returns sum from 1 to index (1-based)
    res = 0
    i = index
    while i > 0
      res += @tree[i]
      i -= i & -i
    end
    res
  end

  def range_query(l, r)
    # returns sum from l to r (1-based)
    return query(r) - query(l - 1)
  end
end

class FenwickTree
  def initialize(size)
    @n = size
    @tree = Array.new(@n + 1, 0)
  end

  def update(index, delta)
    i = index
    while i <= @n
      @tree[i] += delta
      i += i & -i
    end
  end

  def query(index)
    res = 0
    i = index
    while i > 0
      res += @tree[i]
      i -= i & -i
    end
    res
  end

  def range_query(l, r)
    query(r) - query(l - 1)
  end
end

def compute_depth(x)
  depth = 0
  while x != 1
    x = x.to_s(2).count('1')
    depth += 1
  end
  depth

class FenwickTree
  def initialize(size)
    @n = size
    @tree = Array.new(@n + 1, 0)
  end

  def update(index, delta)
    i = index
    while i <= @n
      @tree[i] += delta
      i += i & -i
    end
  end

  def query(index)
    res = 0
    i = index
    while i > 0
      res += @tree[i]
      i -= i & -i
    end
    res
  end

  def range_query(l, r)
    query(r) - query(l - 1)
  end
end

class FenwickTree
  def initialize(size)
    @n = size
    @tree = Array.new(@n + 1, 0)
  end

  def update(index, delta)
    i = index
    while i <= @n
      @tree[i] += delta
      i += i & -i
    end
  end

  def query(index)
    res = 0
    i = index
    while i > 0
      res += @tree[i]
      i -= i & -i
    end
    res
  end

  def range_query(l, r)
    query(r) - query(l - 1)
  end
end

def compute_depth(x)
  depth = 0
  while x != 1
    x = x.to_s(2).count('1')
    depth += 1
  end
  depth
end

def solve(nums, queries)
  n = nums.length
  depths = Array.new(n) { |i| compute_depth(nums[i]) }

  fenwick_trees = Array.new(6) { FenwickTree.new(n) }

  depths.each_with_index do |d, i|
    fenwick_trees[d].update(i + 1, 1)
  end

  answer = []
  queries.each do |q|
    if q[0] == 1
      l = q[1]
      r = q[2]
      k = q[3]
      if k < 0 || k > 5
        answer << 0
        next
      end
      ft = fenwick_trees[k]
      count = ft.range_query(l + 1, r + 1)

      idx = q[1]
      val = q[2]
      old_val = nums[idx]
      old_depth = depths[idx]
      new_depth = compute_depth(val)
      fenwick_trees[old_depth].update(idx + 1, -1)
      fenwick_trees[new_depth].update(idx + 1, 1)
      nums[idx] = val
      depths[idx] = new_depth
    end
  end
  answer
end
